subject: Computer Networking
title: Phishing Attacks
description: A short lesson to help you apply Phishing Attacks.

hook.text: An email from your bank says 'Verify your account now.' The link looks right. But the site is fake.

buildup.text: Phishing uses fake emails, sites, or messages to trick you into entering credentials or downloading malware.

discovery.text: Spear phishing targets specific individuals with personalized details. It's far more convincing than mass campaigns.

twist.text: AI now generates phishing emails without grammar mistakes. The old 'look for typos' advice no longer works.

climax.text: Hardware security keys and passkeys eliminate phishing risk for login. The key only works on the real site.

punchline.text: Phishing doesn't hack systems. It hacks trust.

quiz.question: What makes spear phishing more dangerous than regular phishing?
options: It targets specific individuals with personalized details
It only works on mobile devices
